Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
What's in it for you:

Join a mission driven investment management firm
Grow within a company that invests in YOU
Melbourne CBD + Working from home options

The Role:

We are seeking an AWS Data Engineer to deliver marketing technology capabilities for Advisors. You will collaborate with technical leads, architects, and senior stakeholders across business, technology, and global MarTech teams to drive platform delivery and road map outcomes. In this role, you will design and maintain data pipelines across CRM (Microsoft Dynamics), Marketo, Kaltura, and Adobe Experience Platform (AEP/CDP). You will ensure accurate identity resolution, audience segmentation, and data activation to support personalised marketing journeys.

Key Responsibilities:

Data Pipeline Engineering
Design and optimise ingestion pipelines (batch and streaming) from CRM, Marketo, and Kaltura into CDP using AWS Lambda, S3.
Enable flows to downstream platforms (SFMC, AJO, Marketo, Adobe Target).
Data Modelling
Develop and maintain logical and physical data models to support CDP ingestion and identity resolution.
Ensure schema alignment across CRM, Marketo, event platforms, and CDP.
Leveraging AI Data tools to enhance data led decisions.
Adobe CDP Support & Optimisation
Maintain and enhance Adobe Real-Time CDP schemas, datasets, and identity stitching.
Manage governance labels and compliance standards.
Cross-Platform Enablement
Collaborate with marketing teams to activate audiences across channels.
Configure real-time triggers and penalisation workflows.
Monitoring & Quality Assurance
Implement data quality checks, troubleshoot ingestion issues, and ensure schema alignment.
Security & Compliance
Champion a security-first approach and ensure GDPR/CCPA compliance.

Key Competencies:

Experience with Adobe Experience Platform (AEP/CDP) and MarTech ecosystems - AJO/Marketo/SFMC.
Proficiency in AWS services (Lambda, S3) and API integrations.
Familiarity with CRM systems integration, Marketo, and Kaltura event platform APIs for data extraction and synchronisation.
Strong data modeling skills for designing schemas, mapping attributes, and ensuring compatibility with CDP ingestion and identity stitching.
Diagnosing and resolving intricate problems across APIs, data flows, and platform integrations.
AWS and Adobe certifications (AEP/RTCDP) highly desirable.
Ability to apply AI-driven tools and approaches for automation, penalisation, and data-driven decision-making.
Understanding System Architecture for Scalability and designing resilient solutions that maintain performance under load and ensure high availability.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.

What's in it for you:

Join a mission-driven investment management firm
Grow within a company that invests in YOU
Melbourne CBD + Working from home options

Apply today for a confidential chat.

About Ticketek Entertainment Group​

Ticketek Entertainment Group is a global fan experience Company that tickets, promotes and delivers incredible live experiences that are impossible to forget.  In a distracted world where nothing beats real human moments, We make life better live!

Our Group includes; our Fan Experience Platform (Ticketek) that sells tickets and provides value added services, Event promoting with businesses across Touring (TEG Touring), Sport (TEG Sport), and Family Experiences (TEG Experiences) and our digital business (Ovation) which focuses on delivering seamless data-driven outcomes for our fans and partners

About the Role: 

As a ML engineer, We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Engineer to lead the design and evolution of our world-class recommendation and decision engine. You will be responsible for building a "Single Customer View" platform that integrates real-time data pipelines, Vertex AI, and transactional API solutions. A core focus of this role is leading the transition from legacy architectures to modern, serverless cloud environments, utilizing Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) to ensure the platform is scalable, cost-efficient, cyber and GDPR-compliant



Requirements



What does a day in the life look like?

ML Design & Cloud Architecture

Production-Ready ML & MLOps: Partner with Data Scientists to transition experimental models into robust production services; design automated, scalable pipelines for training, evaluation, and deployment using Vertex AI.
Data Warehouse & SCV: Architect highly scalable Cloud Data Warehouses and Single Customer View logic using Python, Go, and SQL to power real-time decisioning and unified customer profiles.
Smart Orchestration: Manage complex workflow automation using Cloud Composer (Airflow) and Cloud Workflows, prioritizing the migration to fully managed services.

Data Engineering & Analytics

High-Performance Pipelines: Design streaming data applications using Dataflow (Apache Beam), Cloud Run, and Pub/Sub, including API integrations with robust retry mechanisms.
Hybrid Database Management: Build optimized data models in BigQuery and AlloyDB to support both high-speed transactional queries and analytical insights via Looker.
Site Reliability (SRE): Apply SRE principles to diagnose faults in distributed systems and implement advanced monitoring via Google Cloud Operations Suite.

DevOps, Security & FinOps

Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Manage multi-cloud (GCP/AWS) infrastructure using Terraform and GitHub Actions, ensuring all provisioning follows strict version-control and CI/CD principles.
Security & Compliance: Implement secure authentication frameworks (e.g., Workload Identity Federation) and ensure all data handling exceeds GDPR standards.
Cloud Efficiency & Governance: Drive FinOps initiatives through serverless conversions and resource rightsizing while maintaining high-quality documentation for architecture and data lineage.

About You 

Essential experience & skills

Languages: Expert-level Python, SQL, and Shell Scripting; working knowledge of Go.
GCP Ecosystem: Deep expertise in BigQuery, Vertex AI, Dataflow, Cloud Run, GKE, and Pub/Sub.
Infrastructure: Mastery of Terraform and GitHub Actions or Gitlab
Databases: Hands-on experience with Postgres DB's, and NoSQL solutions.
Tools: Expertise in Airflow (DAG development), Looker (LookML), and API management.
Certifications: Professional GCP/AWS Cloud Architect or Data Engineer certifications are highly regarded.


Benefits



Here’s a taste of what TEG offers: 

Complimentary event tickets
Birthday and volunteering leave
Wellbeing discounts & flu vaccinations
Paid parental leave & free employee support (EAP)
Global rewards and recognition
Learning, development & career pathways
A diverse, inclusive, and passionate team

Equal Opportunities

TEG is an equal opportunity employer committed to embrace diversity, respect, and care for our people and communities. 

If there are any adjustments that need to be made to ensure you have a fair and equitable experience in our recruitment process, please advise us when scheduling your interview. 

*Only direct applications will be considered. No recruiters please*

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer (12 month contract) - WildRift Data
Ad Text:
Riot's Data Engineers build products that improve the experience of our players worldwide using petabytes of data and state-of-the-art processing technology. Maximizing the potential of this data is a complex task. 

As a Senior Data Engineer (Contract) joining the Sydney-based team, you will lead the modernization and uplift of our data platform to improve reliability, governance, and scalability. You will drive architectural refactoring across our lakehouse environment, consolidate and standardize data models, and enforce a clear Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion hierarchy. You will implement robust data quality and observability frameworks for business-critical datasets, operationalize data ownership and tiering standards, and elevate metadata completeness and lineage transparency across our data catalog and warehouse ecosystem. Working closely with cross-functional stakeholders, you will help transform the data environment into a trusted, production-grade platform that supports operational decision-making, executive reporting, and advanced analytics. 

This role is based in Sydney and a 12-month contract.

Responsibilities:

Partner with senior engineers to execute architectural improvements across the lakehouse platform.
Implement dimensional data models and standardize semantic/gold reporting layers.
Enforce medallion (Bronze/Silver/Gold) architecture standards.
Build and integrate automated data quality monitoring.
Improve metadata completeness and lineage transparency through catalog updates and automation.
Contribute to CI/CD standards and pipeline reliability improvements.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver production-grade, trusted datasets.
Required Qualifications: 

5+ years of relevant experience building scalable data platforms in production environments.
Strong experience in data modeling and data warehousing, e.g. medallion lakehouse architecture.
Strong hands-on expertise with Databricks, Spark (PySpark/SparkSQL), Delta Lake, Airflow, and Python.
Experience implementing automated data quality checks and validation frameworks.
Experience with dbt or similar transformation frameworks.
Experience delivering on AWS-based data platforms.
Familiarity with metadata/catalog tools (Atlan, Alation, DataHub, Unity Catalog, etc.).
Desired Qualifications:

Fluency in Mandarin Chinese is highly desirable.
Experience contributing to data platform modernization or data maturity uplift initiatives.
Experience working across cross-regional engineering teams.
Experience improving metadata automation or lineage tooling.

For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision-making that prioritizes the delight of players. We will be looking at your past studies, experience, and your personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this could be your role!

Riot Games fosters a player and workplace experience that values teamwork embodied by the Summoner's Code and Community Code. Our culture embraces differences as a strength, and our values are the guiding principles for how we approach work. We are committed to putting diversity and inclusion (D&I) at the center of everything we do, and promoting a fair and collaborative culture where Rioters treat one another with dignity and respect. We encourage you to read more about our value of thriving together and our ongoing work to build the most inclusive company in Gaming.

This role is suited to someone who enjoys building practical AI systems that solve real operational problems, rather than simply consuming APIs. You will contribute to the development of agent-based and workflow-driven automation, integrating AI capabilities with enterprise platforms and business processes.

Location: Sydney 
Length: 12 months (extensions available)
Rate: $900-$1150/day (including Super)

What You'll Do

Design and build AI-enabled applications and automation solutions within a Microsoft ecosystem
Develop and implement agent-based or workflow-driven automation systems
Build solutions using Microsoft Azure services relevant to AI and integration
Integrate AI capabilities with enterprise systems using APIs, event-driven architectures, and messaging patterns
Design and implement decision logic, business rules, and exception handling within AI-driven workflows
Develop scalable, secure, and production-ready AI solutions aligned with enterprise architecture standards
Collaborate with engineering teams and stakeholders to translate business problems into technical solutions
Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
What You'll Bring

Strong hands-on experience building AI-enabled systems, not just consuming AI APIs
Practical experience designing and implementing agent-based systems or workflow-driven automation
Experience working with Microsoft Azure services relevant to AI and integration, including
Experience integrating AI solutions with enterprise platforms via APIs, events, or messaging systems
Ability to model business rules, decision logic, and exception handling clearly within system design
Strong software engineering fundamentals, including version control, testing, and maintainable code practices
Ability to explain technical concepts effectively to non-technical stakeholders
If you’re an AI Engineer who enjoys building real-world AI solutions and intelligent automation, this is an opportunity to contribute to meaningful enterprise transformation.

Ad Text:
Enterprise Data Architect




Permanent, full-time position

Work on high-impact initiatives that drive V/Line operations

Play a key role in a collaborative, forward-thing team

 

V/Line’s purpose is to connect Victorians through safe, reliable, and accessible transport. We believe in giving all Victorians opportunities through travel and transport – connecting them to jobs, education, family and friends, services, tourism and trade.

V/Line is experiencing significant growth and we’re delivering more services, more trains, cheaper fares, and huge projects. With more than 3700 weekly train and coach services, an expansive freight network and unprecedented rail investment, millions of Victorians depend on us to deliver. You could be one of our 3100 committed team members who are up to the challenge.




About the role

As our new Enterprise Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining V/Line’s target data architecture and ensuring platforms, models and governance align with business needs. You’ll be the technical authority designing scalable, integrated data solutions that connect legacy systems with modern technologies to support enterprise-wide decision making.

Key Accountabilities include:

 Define and maintain enterprise data architecture principles, standards and patterns aligned to business strategy.

 Develop data models and integration patterns across structured and unstructured data.

 Design solutions that connect legacy systems with modern platforms to create a unified data ecosystem.

 Architect AI/ML platforms and infrastructure to enable advanced analytics.

 Build capability uplift roadmaps to transition from current to target-state architecture.

 Establish data architecture governance to ensure consistent delivery and compliance.

 Assess emerging technologies and recommend strategic adoption opportunities.

 Work closely with data engineers, analysts and governance teams to ensure successful delivery.

 Develop reusable reference architectures and design patterns.

 Ensure architectures support advanced analytics, ML and regulatory requirements.

 Translate business goals into clear architectural requirements through stakeholder engagement.

 

About you 

To be considered for this role, applicants must meet the following key selection criteria within their application, as this forms part of our merit-based recruitment process:

Must have 10+ years of experience successfully working in data architecture, engineering, or related roles.

Demonstrated experience designing architecture solutions for complex, siloed environments on cloud-native data platforms (AWS, Azure, Databricks, Snowflake).

Proven experience designing practical architectural solutions from complex business requirements, for both structured and unstructured data.

Strong understanding of data governance frameworks and regulatory requirements

Established experience creating architecture transition roadmaps, as well as designing data, AI and ML platforms and infrastructure.

Industry-recognised certification in AWS and/or Databricks (highly desirable).

Tertiary qualifications in IT, Business Management, or other related fields (highly desirable).

Experience working in similar industries e.g. Transport/Manufacturing/Logistics/Utilities (highly desirable)




Salary for this position is commensurate to the successful candidate’s aligned skills and experience to the position.




Why V/Line

In addition to free myki travel, we offer:

Community impact – Make a genuine difference. Work in a role that connects Victorians, every day.

Huge potential – We’re transforming, and you can too. Be a change maker, recognised for your contribution and creativity.

Dynamic environment – V/Line operates in a constantly changing space. Join a fast-paced and exciting organisation.

Collaborative team – You’ll be part of a broader supportive team. Surround yourself with talented, passionate peers.

Flexible working – We support flexible working. Work in a way that supports you to thrive in your role.




We are committed to ensuring a comfortable and accessible recruitment experience. If you need any adjustments or have specific accessibility requirements at any stage of the process, please contact our Talent team at TalentSupport@vline.com.au or 1800 800 007 for confidential support.




As an equal opportunity employer, V/Line is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace and network that reflects the communities we serve. We accept and value the contributions of all our employees regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, age or disability. We are also proud to be endorsed by Work 180 as a great place for women to work, second year in a row.




V/Line are proud to be a part of the Department of Transport & Planning's Yani Bangal Transport and Planning Portfolio First Peoples Careers Strategy 2023-2028. We take this opportunity to welcome and encourage applications from First Peoples.  




At V/Line, safety is our priority. Successful applicants will undergo relevant pre-employment and medical checks to meet our safeworking standards.

Talenza have been engaged by an Australian clean energy company to find an Information Architect to work across a data uplift program of work. The successful candidate will be an experienced data/information architect who has worked with modern microsoft data technologies.

This role requires 3 days per week in the Brisbane office and is only open to candidates currently based in Brisbane with full working rights.



Benefits

Contract opportunity
Competitive day rate up to $1175 + GST
Hybrid working environment (3 days per week in office)
Amazing leadership group and office/team culture
Global ASX listed enterprise
Clean Energy Organisation





Key Requirements

Strong experience designing and governing enterprise data and information architectures that support secure, trusted and accessible data across operational and analytical platforms.
Expertise in cloud data platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure, including services such as Azure Synapse, Data Lake, Data Factory, Azure SQL and Event Hub.
Proven capability in data architecture and engineering, including data modelling, data warehouse/lakehouse design, ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming data and data migration.
Experience designing enterprise integration architectures, including APIs, messaging/service bus patterns and integration between operational systems and analytics platforms.
Strong knowledge of enterprise analytics and reporting platforms, particularly Power BI, including platform architecture, performance monitoring and user adoption.
Experience developing and implementing information architecture and governance, including information classification, taxonomy, ontology and enterprise information asset structuring.
Demonstrated expertise in data governance, data quality, privacy and compliance, including data cataloguing and lineage tools such as Microsoft Purview.
Familiarity with advanced analytics platforms and technologies, including AI/ML capabilities, LLM use cases and programming languages such as Python, R and SQL.
Experience working with DevOps and modern delivery practices, including code control, CI/CD pipelines, platform monitoring and operational performance management.




Thank you for your interest, at Talenza we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity. We encourage people with disabilities and those from diverse backgrounds to apply. However, please note that we are only able to proceed with those who have full rights to work in Australia. Please apply directly or for a quicker response, contact Lewis Babcock (Talenza) through LinkedIn.

Ad Text:

Pay rate: $800 - $900 per day + Super

Job Title: EOI - AI Implementation Engineer (Start in FY2027)
Ad Text:
Commencing New Financial Year (June/July 2026)

Ready to turn GenAI ideas into real-world impact? Temple & Webster is where design meets technology. As Australia’s number one online retailer for furniture and homewares, we are constantly reimagining how customers discover, shop, and connect online.

We are expanding our AI capability and looking for a seasoned AI Implementation Engineer to join us for the start of the new FY27 (June/July 2026). This isn't a role for theorists or "prompt-only" enthusiasts, we need an applied developer who has spent the last few years in the trenches of commercial AI development.

The Role
This position sits firmly within our Tech and AI stack. It is designed for a software veteran who has spent at least the last two years specialized in GenAI within a commercial setting. You’ll be moving beyond the "demo" phase, building hardened, innovative GenAI and agentic solutions that are embedded directly into our core business operations.
What You Will Be Doing

Engineer & Architect: Build and launch GenAI-powered features across the business, moving them from proof-of-concepts to high-availability production environments.
Agentic Workflows: Design AI agents that don't just "chat," but execute, integrating them into our core operational systems to remove manual toil.
Scale & Optimize: Stay close to the code (Python, APIs, Cloud) to ensure our AI systems meet the performance, latency, and cost-efficiency needs of a high-growth retailer.
Operational Excellence: Implement and abide by LLMOps best practices, including rigorous evaluation frameworks and observability. Prototype aggressively and ship innovative AI solutions while maintaining the rigorous safety, ethical, and architectural security required for enterprise-scale deployment
Entrepreneurial and Innovative Spirit: Bring capabilities to redefine/discover what’s possible in a high-functioning AI ecosystem.
What We Are Looking For

Minimum 2 years of hands-on experience building and maintaining GenAI solutions in a commercial AI team.
4+ years of professional software engineering and/or professional coding experience, with deep proficiency in Python and SQL.
Proven Production Pedigree: You have successfully deployed and scaled GenAI solutions into live environments..
Cloud Fluency: Experience working within cloud ecosystems, ideally Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Bonus Points: Experience or a background in data science/analytics before moving into AI engineering.
Our Selection Process
We value communication as much as coding. Our streamlined process for this July intake is as follows:

30-60 Second Video Cover Letter: Tell us about one GenAI solution you’ve shipped to production and the biggest hurdle you overcame.
Initial Phone Screen: A brief conversation with our team to discuss your experience and the FY27 roadmap.
Technical Zoom Interview: A deep dive with our AI team into your engineering approach and problem-solving skills.
Why You Will Enjoy It Here

A New FY Start: Join us in June/July as we kick off a fresh roadmap of high-impact AI initiatives.
Real Ownership: A tight-knit team where your code directly influences how Australia shops.
The Perks: Beautiful St Peters office, Thursday happy hours, and catered lunches three days a week.
Balance: Hybrid working, daily mindfulness sessions, paid birthday leave, and staff discounts.
How to Apply Submit your CV along with your 30-60 second video clip via the link below. We are looking for candidates who can explain complex AI concepts with clarity and enthusiasm.

Ready to build the future? 

Imagine joining the team helping to shape the backbone of Australia’s AI revolution. For more than 40 years, Benmax has been trusted to deliver exceptional mechanical services, and today we’re contributing to some of the most ambitious, future‑focused projects in the country. This is an opportunity to be part of something significant while advancing your career in a rapidly evolving field. 

 

As we continue to invest in innovation, efficiency and continuous improvement, Benmax is now recruiting for a newly established AI Specialist within our Corporate Services Division. 

 

This role will be instrumental in leading the rollout, adoption and optimisation of AI tools and processes across the business. You’ll help teams work smarter, enhance operational performance, and support the delivery of Benmax’s strategic objectives as we evolve and grow. 

About the role

You bring together strategic thinking, strong governance capability and confident stakeholder engagement with a deep understanding of artificial intelligence, data and emerging technologies. You can translate organisational priorities into practical, ethical and scalable AI initiatives, working closely with teams across the business to deliver meaningful outcomes.

As a trusted advisor, you partner with leaders and operational teams to identify where AI can enhance processes, improve decision‑making and unlock new efficiencies. You bridge the gap between business needs and technology, helping stakeholders understand what’s possible and guiding the responsible adoption of AI solutions.

You can expect to:

Develop and implement an AI adoption roadmap aligned with business needs
Identify opportunities for AI driven efficiencies and automation
Design, build and maintain AI agents, workflows and dashboards
Deliver training and support to build confidence and capability in AI tools
Ensure strong governance, data quality, privacy, security and auditability
Act as the central point of contact for AI related queries and improvements

What you’ll bring

Essential

Tertiary qualification in Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, Project Management, Business, or another discipline relevant to the role.
Strong experience in AI implementation, business process improvement, or technology‑driven solutions
Solid understanding of AI technologies, tools, and practical business applications
Ability to analyse processes and translate business needs into effective AI solutions
Excellent communication skills, including the ability to train and support stakeholders
Permanent working rights in Australia

Desirable

Certification in AI or machine learning

About us 

Benmax is known for quality, innovation, and a genuine commitment to its people. We invest in professional development, encourage fresh ideas, and value collaboration. If you want to work where your expertise is recognised and you can see the impact of your work, you’ll fit right in.

Want to know more about us? Visit benmax.com.au or follow us on social media.

How to apply

Apply through the online application portal and include:

A one-page Cover Letter outlining your suitability
A current Resume/CV with your details, qualifications, and experience

Please note: Applicants must have permanent rights to work in Australia (we cannot offer sponsorship)

Ad Text:
Job Summary / Overview

An experienced Analytics Engineer is required to support asset analysis and modelling through the development of structured data models and reporting solutions. The role focuses on transforming complex SAP PM and CMMS maintenance datasets into reliable analytical outputs that support planners reliability engineers and maintenance stakeholders. This position collaborates closely with developers and operational teams to deliver scalable data solutions and reporting insights.

Key Responsibilities

* Design and maintain curated Snowflake SQL data models and reporting views supporting asset performance and maintenance analytics
* Transform structure and validate SAP PM and CMMS datasets including equipment functional locations work orders maintenance plans and items
* Develop and enhance Power BI datasets and reports to support maintenance strategy and performance monitoring
* Drive continuous improvement in data quality through investigation testing and issue resolution
* Maintain documentation governance and traceability across analytical models and reporting solutions
* Collaborate with technical teams and operational stakeholders to deliver effective analytical outputs
Required Qualifications
* Degree in Engineering Data Science Computer Science or related technical discipline or equivalent industry experience
* Strong SQL capability using Snowflake or SQL Server with experience in data modelling and transformation
* Experience developing structured analytical datasets and reporting solutions such as Power BI
* Experience working with SAP PM or CMMS maintenance data structures highly desirable
* Experience within mining Fixed Plant or Heavy Mobile Equipment maintenance environments advantageous
* Python experience for data transformation or automation desirable
Skills & Competencies
* Strong SQL data modelling and transformation skills
* Experience developing Power BI reporting solutions
* Understanding of SAP PM or CMMS maintenance data structures
* Strong attention to data integrity documentation and governance
* Ability to collaborate with both technical teams and operational stakeholders

Working Conditions (Optional)

Perth CBD location

We are looking for a Junior DSP/Edge‑AI Engineer with a strong foundation in digital signal processing and an interest in machine learning and embedded systems.

You will work on an end‑to‑end edge AI pipeline:

data collection → signal processing → feature extraction → ML model training → quantization → deployment onto embedded hardware (FPGA/ARM)

This is a hands-on R&D role suitable for a graduate or engineer with 0–2 years of experience who is excited to learn advanced topics such as AI acceleration, FPGA deployment, and real‑time signal processing.




Key Responsibilities

1. Core DSP Engineering

Process and analyse raw sensor waveform data

Implement DSP algorithms in Python (NumPy, SciPy) or MATLAB

Design and evaluate filters (FIR/IIR), FFT/STFT, matched filters, spectrograms

Build and optimise feature‑extraction pipelines

Assess signal quality, noise behaviour and sampling strategies

2. Machine Learning

Prepare and label datasets

Train ML models using PyTorch or TensorFlow

Conduct 1D/2D CNN experiments

Assist with model optimisation (pruning, quantisation)

Convert models to ONNX and TFLite formats

3. Edge Deployment

(No prior FPGA experience required — full training provided)

Implement preprocessing/postprocessing on ARM processors

Run optimised inference on embedded devices

Use FPGA toolchains such as Xilinx Vitis AI

Support ML model compilation for FPGA accelerators (DPU / HLS flow)

4. Engineering Workflow

Write clean, maintainable Python and C/C++ code

Use Git for version control

Document pipelines, experiments, and results

Participate in design meetings and daily stand‑ups




Educational Background

Bachelor’s degree in Electronics Engineering, with a strong focus on Digital Signal Processing.

Digital Signal Processing Fundamentals

Sampling theory, aliasing, Nyquist, quantization

FFT, STFT, and spectrograms

FIR/IIR filters, convolution, correlation

Noise analysis, windowing, SNR

Programming Skills

Python (NumPy, SciPy, matplotlib)

Basic experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow

Exposure to C/C++ and Linux command line

Mathematical Foundations

Linear algebra

Probability & statistics

Basic optimisation techniques

Soft Skills

Curious, proactive, and eager to learn

Strong analytical & debugging ability

Effective in small R&D teams

Willing to adopt new tools (FPGA, ML frameworks, embedded platforms)

Role Title: Senior AI Platform Engineer

Be part of one of the most innovative companies in Australia with industry leading technology
A place to truly be yourself and thrive in your career from the beginning
Recognised and endorsed as an employer for all women by Work180 – ranking #35 in 2025

About Us

At Sportsbet, we’re united behind our purpose to Bring Excitement to Life for over 2.4 million customers. We do this by delivering fun, challenging the status quo, and living our values. As part of the global Flutter Group, we’re a major player internationally, but in Melbourne, Sydney and Darwin, we’re a tightknit team of 1,300+ teammates (with over half in Tech & Data Science).

Our commitment to responsible gambling is genuine and demonstrated through the wide range of responsible gambling initiatives and tools. We’re proud to be the leaders in responsible gambling in our industry, not just locally, but globally.

We are one of the top workplaces for Women through WORK180. WORK180 is a leading platform that independently assesses employers on policies, benefits and practices that support women and underrepresented groups at work. We remain committed to making Sportsbet the best place to work and encourage you to explore our employer profile on the WORK180 website to learn more about what we offer.


About The Role

This is an exciting opportunity to join a high-performing platform team focused on evolving and scaling Sportsbet’s cloud and AI capabilities. Reporting into the Platform Manager – Cloud, you will play a key role in designing, building, and operating Sportsbet’s emerging AI Platforms.

You will work closely with cloud engineers, architects, and application teams on core platform initiatives spanning cloud architecture, automation, governance, and cost optimisation. With cloud and AI as the primary focus, you will help define and implement secure, scalable AI platform architectures and patterns using AWS cloud services and AI/ML products.

You will also drive efficiency by collaborating cross-functionally to embed AI capabilities into core systems and workflows, minimising duplicated effort and accelerating delivery across teams. Through reusable solution architectures, automation, and platform-first thinking, you will help lift overall cloud and AI maturity across the organisation.

In addition, you will mentor and support other engineers, promote modern engineering practices, and champion cloud-native and AI-first approaches while maintaining a strong focus on operational excellence, cost optimisation, and long-term platform sustainability.

About You

While we are big on development and offer on the job training, we want to set you up for success. Along with a great attitude, it would be ideal that you have:

Deep expertise in AWS cloud services, (IaC, Cloud networking, security etc.) with strong hands-on experience in AI and ML.
Proven experience architecting and deploying AI-centric architectures in the cloud, including scalable, secure, and cost-optimised solutions.
Deep code development expertise in Python, with experience integrating AI services via APIs.
Excellent leadership skills with the ability to communicate with influence.
Passionate about coaching and mentoring others.
Ability to adapt to shifting priorities.


The Perks

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus for permanent staff members, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, an additional week’s leave!)
Hybrid working policy (Monday and Friday from home) and a $1,050 home office allowance for new starters.
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.
Hack Days - where your creativity and collaborative ideas lead to exciting new ideas and projects for our customers.
Access to outstanding career development programs, including expert-led internal training and external learning opportunities (staff allowance for personal development)
Weekly concierge service including free massages and nail appointments – all onsite in our Melbourne office!
Free breakfast, daily fresh fruit and weekly rotating socials, from Thursday office drinks to fun team events
End of trip facilities with onsite table tennis and pool tables
Discounted health insurance and free flu jabs yearly.

We review applications within the first two weeks, so apply now! Check out our Life@Sportsbet Instagram, LinkedIn, and careers website for more information about our inclusive culture.  

Our Focus on Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

We don’t just support diversity – we celebrate it! This is a workplace where you can thrive and feel safe to bring your whole authentic self to work.  

What’s the Green Tick Feeling?  

Well, it’s different for everyone, yet unites us all. It’s the feeling you get when you succeed, no matter what success looks like to you. It's all the little and big things that make you feel like you’re thriving when working at Sportsbet.   
   
We’re an inclusive employer who welcomes you for who you are, as you are – so, if you require adjustments to the recruitment process, please let us know in your application. We also know flex means different things to different people, so let us know how we can support you to be your best.   

If you think you’d be a great fit but don’t necessarily meet everything in the job description, please still get in touch.

About the role:

As a Data & AI Practice Manager, you provide oversight and mentoring across a multidisciplinary team spanning data engineering, analytics/BI, platform operations, and AI/ML engineering. You will collaborate with other Practice Managers and technical specialists to create consistency in client management, delivery approach, and resource allocation for projects and operational needs. This is a directly chargeable and team leadership role, accountable for client engagement and business development, scoping, solution design, and successful delivery of data platform and AI initiatives.

Drive Comunet's Data & AI Practice and engage in business development.

Maintain personal knowledge and expertise in data platforms, analytics, AI/ML practices, frameworks, and emerging technologies to lead and deliver meaningful change.

Manage consultants and client managers in concert with resource and project managers across Comunet.

Work closely with internal stakeholders, external service providers, clients, and suppliers.

Provide status updates and progress reports as required by leadership team members.

Actively manage a personal portfolio of Data & AI clients and opportunities.




Key responsibilities of the role:

Own the delivery of data platform programs: modernisation, migrations, data integration (ETL/ELT), quality and governance using Data/DevOps practices.

Lead solution scoping and estimation for Data & AI projects, shaping proposals/SOWs, workshops, and delivery plans in collaboration with Project Management.

Guide clients on AI use-cases, feasibility, data readiness, and value realisation; oversee discovery, experimentation, and production phases.

Meet personal and team chargeable targets in line with set budgets.

Drive personal and team consulting capabilities including clear training and development plans.

Influence innovation, architectural and technical design solutions for both Comunet and our clients.

Drive continuous improvement of Comunet engagement and consulting processes, both technical and non-technical.

Engage in relevant vendor or community events and user groups

Be accountable for delegated HR functions for technical specialists as required.

Act as senior point of escalation for data and AI challenges and client issues.

Provide monthly commentary/reporting on team and individual performance, resourcing status/risks, and client portfolio development.




Information Handling Responsibilities:

Understand information management responsibilities associated with the role and adhere to relevant policies and procedures.

Implement data classification, retention, and access controls across client engagements; ensure appropriate handling of PII and sensitive data.

Understand and advise on emergent legislation, guidelines, and practices for Data and AI across security, and ethical concerns.

Promote privacy-by-design and security-by-design as relevant to each client.


Key required skills and qualifications:

A minimum of 10+ years' experience delivering professional IT services, including 5+ years in a combination of Data/Analytics/AI leadership or client management.

You will need to hold an appropriate formal qualification in Information Technology, Data Science or relevant discipline or equivalent experience.

Role will require interstate travel and out of hours commitments.

Proven ability to translate business objectives into Data & AI strategies, roadmaps, and measurable outcomes.

Strong oral and written communication skills for engaging clients, leading workshops, and producing clear documentation.

Effective project management skills: planning, execution, timelines, budgets, and resource coordination in partnership with Project Management.

Leadership and team management: building high-performing teams, fostering collaboration, and aligning goals.

Client Relationship Management: Building and maintaining strong relationships with clients is essential. This involves understanding client needs, providing excellent service, and ensuring client satisfaction

Continuous learning mindset: staying updated with industry trends, technologies, and best practices.

Extensive experience with data platforms (Snowflake, DataBricks, Fabric) and cloud providers (e.g., AWS and Microsoft Azure native data and development services).

Hands-on understanding of data integration (ETL/ELT), streaming, data modelling, warehousing/lakehouse architectures, and BI/analytics (e.g., Power BI).

Experience with data governance and cataloguing (e.g., Microsoft Purview) including lineage, metadata, and policy enforcement.

Exposure to AI/ML engineering: feature pipelines, model training/evaluation, MLOps (MLflow/Azure ML, SageMaker), monitoring and drift management.

Experience in a consulting organisation highly regarded.




Benefits

Competitive market salary

Flexible working conditions

Mobility allowance

Training opportunities

Birthday leave

A package relevant to experience and credentials will be tailored to suit the candidate.




About Comunet:

At Comunet, we love technology. But more than that, we love helping clients use technology to build better businesses. We've been on an everchanging technology journey for over 30 years and have seen the challenges first-hand with hundreds of businesses and what this means for them - in trying to maintain currency, with costs, security and the inevitable 'future-proofing' in mind.

We have deep expertise across Infrastructure, Networking, Cloud, Modern Application Development and embedded Cyber Security and drive a continuous improvement and innovation program across all of our teams to ensure we maintain our position as a leading provider of technology services.

We offer a published Employee Value Proposition (https://comunet.com.au/careers/employee-offer/) to expand on salary and package offers includes dedicated training budgets and time allocation, fully stocked pantry and fridge, regular team and whole of organization events and opportunities for flexible working and purchased additional annual leave.

We believe in making work a fun place to be, and we work hard to maintain our culture while providing exceptional service to our clients.




How to Apply

We'd love a cover letter with your CV, and as this is active ongoing recruitment process the position may be filled with the right applicant before the published closing date - so encourage applications as early as possible.

By submitting an application to Comunet, you acknowledge that we will undertake workforce screening activities including, but not limited to: Reference checks, National Crime and Right to Work Checks and Identity and certification verification.

Only candidates that are short listed will be contacted.

Senior Integration & Data Engineers responsible for designing, delivering and maintaining secure Azure-based integration solutions and ETL/ELT pipelines for a leading University in Sydney.

Key Responsibilities

Deliver integration and data solutions aligned with enterprise architecture principles and the University's IT governance model, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and security.
Ensure ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines are reliable, monitored, and meet SLAs while supporting analytics, reporting, and business intelligence needs.
Apply DevSecOps practices to enable secure, automated, and resilient deployment of data and integration services, including infrastructure-as-code.
Support service delivery in line with ITIL practices, focusing on reliability, incident response, change management, and continuous improvement.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and external partners, and maintain clear system and user documentation for operational continuity.
Provide mentorship and capability uplift to team members while driving continuous improvement through best practices, modern tooling, and automation.

Qualifications, skills & experience

Postgraduate qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline, and equivalent relevant experience, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training.
Preferred: Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a closely related field.
Proven experience designing and delivering enterprise-grade, cloud-native data and integration solutions on Microsoft Azure.
Demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and governing integration and data solutions in complex enterprise environments using Microsoft Azure services, with strong capability in ETL/ELT, data warehousing, data lake architectures, SQL/NoSQL, and defining data engineering and modelling requirements for analytics and BI.
Hands‑on expertise with Azure Functions, Logic Apps, ADF, APIM, Azure SQL, Data Lake, Service Bus, Event Grid, Azure DevOps, and Microsoft Fabric.
Proficient in Python and C# and experienced with unit testing, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps, and writing secure, high‑performance code while applying FinOps principles for cost optimisation.
Proven ability to work across complex organisational structures, build strong cross‑functional relationships, apply data‑driven and risk‑based approaches to prioritise engineering activities, drive continuous improvement and innovation, and communicate technical concepts clearly to stakeholders.

Benefits

Fast paced environment with opportunity for professional growth
Generous training and development opportunities
Friendly and collaborative working environment

We help large organisations solve operational problems through the intelligent use of AI and technology. Our engineers don't just build, they understand the business problem first, then build the right thing.

We're growing our engineering team and looking for multiple AI Engineers across mid and senior levels.

The role

You'll design and build AI applications and agentic systems for some of Australia's largest organisations in mining, energy, finance and government. You'll work directly with clients, which means you need to be as comfortable in a conversation about business problems as you are writing code.

This isn't a heads-down technical role. You'll be involved from requirements through to production, with close engagement with the customers and stakeholders.

What we're looking for

We're not expecting years of AI experience, everything is too new and moving too fast for that to be realistic. What we do expect is a strong engineering foundation, demonstrate willingness to learn fast, and a genuine interest in where AI is going.

You'll have a background in software development (Python, Typescript & AWS experience is a plus), a track record of delivering real solutions in commercial environments, and the communication skills to explain what you're building and why it matters to both technical and non-technical audiences.

If you're already experimenting with AI tools, building things in your own time, and following the space closely, that is a positive.

Why Horizon Digital

We work on real, production-grade AI projects with major Australian enterprises and we deliver fast. You'll be part of a team on the leading edge of AI in WA.
